How Trump Took the U.S. to War With Iran Before Iran War, Netanyahu privately briefed Trump in the White House Situation Room. He presented a bold, “quick win” plan to strike Iran, destroy missiles, avoid retaliation, and even trigger regime change. Trump liked it immediately: “Sounds good to me.” But U.S. intelligence pushed back hard: Military strikes = doable. Regime change = “farcical” (CIA). Advisers warned Israel was overselling, but Trump brushed off concerns: “Regime change… that’s their problem.” He focused on killing leadership + crushing Iran’s military, believing it would be fast and decisive. Trump asked each adviser for input: Vance: “Bad idea, but I’ll support you”. Rubio: Only support if goal = military, not regime change. Military: outlined risks, but didn’t oppose. Legal: said it was permissible. Political team: warned of backlash, but deferred. No one firmly blocked the decision. And here we are. Source: NYT

↩️ Reply to OSINT Visioner 🇺🇸 BIG | US Navy requested record amounts of funding in the budget for the 2027 fiscal year, planning to spend about $3 billion on Tomahawk cruise missiles and $4.3 billion on new SM-6 anti-aircraft missiles. It's worth noting a rather sharp jump: in fact, the request for "Tomahawks" has increased by 1200% compared to last year and is dictated by the gradual depletion of arsenals during the current campaign in the Middle East. As part of the operation against Iran, the US Navy has already spent about 850 Tomahawk missiles, which is almost a quarter of the operational reserve and a new record since the 1991 Persian Gulf War. The new purchases are intended not only to make up for the shortage, but also to prepare the fleet for a potential confrontation with China in the Pacific Ocean. However, it is noted that the technical complexity of the task lies in the capabilities of the American defense industry: the supply chain for "Tomahawks" is currently overloaded due to the number of highly specialized suppliers. To circumvent these limitations, the administration plans to allocate a significant portion of defense spending through the budget reconciliation mechanism, which will allow for the rapid approval of funding and then the conclusion of large multi-year contracts for purchases - including hundreds of Tomahawks and SM-6s - with the distribution of production over several years ahead. All this will give defense giants, such as Raytheon (the manufacturer of both systems), guarantees for expanding workshops and hiring staff, as well as opportunities for more mass production in the future. Human Shield, Human Chain & "TARGET?" —Serbia 1999, Iran 2026 - Same Script, Same War Crimes & Criminals Serbia, 1999: ordinary citizens standing on bridges holding hand-drawn signs with a bullseye and word "TARGET?", children and their parents making peace signs, rock concerts on bridges over Danube... Thousands forming human chains around power plants, daring NATO aircraft to do what NATO aircraft were, in fact, doing: destroying civilian infrastructure and calling the dead " collateral damage ." The western press had a ready-made headline for all of it: " Milošević is using his own people as human shields. " Now look at today's Western presstitute coverage. April 7, 2026, day 39 of Operation Epic Epstein Fury. Israel has acknowledged attacking Iranian railway lines and bridges, the U.S. has struck the oil export hub of Kharg Island, and at least 18 civilians were killed in a single province in Tuesday's strikes alone. CBS News The IDF, whose strikes hit bridges in Kashan, Qom, Tabriz and Karaj, warned Iranians in Farsi on X to " Refrain from using and traveling by train throughout Iran " — acknowledging that it was about to bomb trains — while most Iranians couldn't see the warning because the internet has been shut off. NBC News And when Iranians gathered around power plants to protect them? Western outlets reached for the same script, untouched since 1999: "Iran's regime calls on youth to form human shields." Mirror NY Post The playbook has not been updated, not even a bit. Here is the factual record: According to the Iranian Red Crescent Society , 65 schools and 32 medical facilities have been targeted since the war started, and more than 10,000 civilian sites have been damaged. Wikipedia Iran's Ministry of Health reports 220 children killed , 41 ambulances damaged, and 25 health facilities struck. CNN Sharif University of Technology — Iran's MIT — hit yesterday - as the 30-odd something Iranian University. A double tap strike hit an elementary school in Minab early in the war, killing 168 children and 14 teachers . In total, over 600 schools were damaged so far. Swissinfo And Trump's own words on the bridges he is destroying: "The biggest bridge in Iran comes tumbling down, never to be used again — Much more to follow!" NPR Today he escalated: "A whole civilisation will die tonight, never to be brought back again." euronews Sociopathic NATO spokesman Jamie Shea, May 1999, at least had the institutional discipline to call it " civilian inconvenience ." The current administration is not bothering with the euphemisms. The people standing on Belgrade bridges in 1999 understood something the western press still cannot process: If you are prepared to bomb infrastructure with civilians present — having first issued a warning you know most people cannot receive — then calling those civilians "human shields" is not a legal or moral argument. It is the press release you file afterwards to avoid using the words "war crime." Experts said targeting civilian infrastructure is a war crime. Obviously. The civilians standing between the bombs and the powerplants and the bridges that their lives depend upon, are not shields. They have correctly identified who the aggressor is – which, if you pay close attention to the articles used as "sources" in this text, and thousands of other across the MSM - Western media cannot. And to top it all off - Iranians, unlike Serbs 27 years ago - aren't risking their youth, their children - look at today's photos and videos. Who are the actual war criminals? It's not just our psychopaths in charge, and our media - We, the "collective West", are. All of us. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics
